title: Google Chrome Skia Out-of-Bounds Write and V8 Type Confusion Zero-Days Actively Exploited in the Wild: CISA Adds Both to KEV Catalog with Federal Patch Deadline (CVE-2026-3909, CVE-2026-3910)
affected_products: Any Chromium-based browser on Windows, macOS, Linux, ChromeOS, Android

sources.name: The Hacker News - Google Fixes Two Chrome Zero-Days Exploited in the Wild
BleepingComputer - Google fixes two new Chrome zero-days exploited in attacks
CISA Alert - Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
The Register - Google rushes Chrome update to fix zero-days under attack
Malwarebytes - Google patches two Chrome zero-days under active attack
CyberSecurityNews - Chrome Zero-Day Vulnerabilities Actively Exploited
Vulert - Google Fixes Two Chrome Zero-Days CVE-2026-3909 and CVE-2026-3910
SOC Prime - CVE-2026-3910: Chrome V8 Zero-Day Used for In-the-Wild Attacks
Windows Forum - CISA KEV Adds Critical Skia and Chromium V8 Flaws
OffSeq Threat Radar - CVE-2026-3909 Out-of-Bounds Write in Chrome
Chrome Releases Blog - March 2026 Security Update

timeline.event: Google security team discovers both zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3909 in Skia and CVE-2026-3910 in V8) being actively exploited in the wild
Google releases emergency Chrome security update (version 146.0.7680.75/76) patching both zero-days
Microsoft releases emergency Edge security update (version 126.0.2592.68) incorporating the same Chromium patches
CISA adds CVE-2026-3909 and CVE-2026-3910 to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog with a federal patch deadline of March 27, 2026
Active exploitation continues; all Chromium-based browser vendors begin rolling out patches

simple_summary.what_happened: Google discovered two critical security holes in its Chrome browser that hackers were already exploiting before Google even released a fix. The first flaw (CVE-2026-3909) is in Skia, the graphics engine that draws everything you see on screen. The second flaw (CVE-2026-3910) is in V8, the JavaScript engine that runs code on websites. Together, these vulnerabilities allow attackers to completely take over a victim's computer just by getting them to visit a malicious website — no file download or click required. Both flaws were immediately added to the US government's list of dangerous vulnerabilities that federal agencies must patch urgently.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Anyone using Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, Brave, Opera, Vivaldi, or any other Chromium-based browser on Windows, macOS, Linux, ChromeOS, or Android. With Chrome alone commanding over 65% of global browser market share, this affects hundreds of millions of users worldwide. Organizations with unpatched browsers are at immediate risk of complete endpoint compromise via drive-by attacks.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attacker sets up a malicious or compromised website containing crafted HTML and JavaScript designed to trigger both vulnerabilities simultaneously. Step 2: Victim visits the malicious site — this can happen via phishing email link, malvertising banner, or watering hole attack on a trusted website. Step 3: CVE-2026-3910 (V8 type confusion) is triggered first: the JavaScript engine misinterprets a data type, causing out-of-bounds memory access inside the browser's sandbox. Step 4: CVE-2026-3909 (Skia out-of-bounds write) is chained in sequence: the graphics engine overwrites memory outside its allocated buffer, corrupting security boundaries and enabling sandbox escape. Step 5: With the sandbox escaped, the attacker's code executes on the victim's underlying operating system with the user's full privileges — enabling malware installation, credential theft, ransomware deployment, or lateral movement across the corporate network.
what_you_should_do: Immediately update Google Chrome to version 146.0.7680.75 or higher (Help menu > Check for updates, then restart Chrome)
Update Microsoft Edge to version 126.0.2592.68 or higher and restart
Update all other Chromium-based browsers (Brave, Opera, Vivaldi) to their latest versions
Use endpoint management tools (Intune, SCCM, Jamf) to push browser updates enterprise-wide and verify all endpoints are patched
Until patches are confirmed deployed, restrict user browsing to approved domains via web proxy or firewall policies
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: These vulnerabilities require zero user interaction beyond visiting a webpage — the attack can be launched invisibly through malvertising on legitimate sites, making it nearly impossible for users to protect themselves without patching. The combination of a Skia graphics flaw chained with a V8 JavaScript engine flaw creates a reliable sandbox escape path, giving attackers full operating system access. With CISA adding both to the KEV catalog, US federal agencies have until March 27 to patch. For enterprises, a single employee visiting a compromised website on an unpatched browser can provide attackers with a foothold into the entire corporate network.

tactics.description: Attackers deliver the exploit via malicious or compromised websites. Simply visiting the page in an unpatched Chrome/Chromium browser triggers the exploit chain with no additional user interaction required.
CVE-2026-3910 (V8 type confusion) and CVE-2026-3909 (Skia out-of-bounds write) are chained to achieve reliable code execution within and ultimately outside the browser sandbox.
The Skia out-of-bounds write (CVE-2026-3909) is used to escape the browser sandbox, elevating execution context from the sandboxed renderer process to the full user-level OS context.
Exploit JavaScript is heavily obfuscated to bypass browser-based threat detection and security tools that inspect web content.

tactics.technique_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys
tactics.description: After sandbox escape, threat actors establish persistence on compromised endpoints through registry modifications or scheduled tasks before deploying second-stage payloads.

detection_guidance.summary: Detection focuses on identifying unpatched Chrome/Chromium browser versions via asset inventory, monitoring for abnormal browser process behaviors indicative of exploitation attempts, and tracking network-level indicators from known exploit delivery infrastructure.

log_sources.description: Monitor chrome.exe and msedge.exe for anomalous child process spawning, unexpected DLL loading, and unusual memory allocation patterns that indicate sandbox escape attempts.
Query all endpoints for installed Chrome/Edge versions. Any instance below Chrome 146.0.7680.75 or Edge 126.0.2592.68 is actively vulnerable.
Look for connections to newly registered domains, domains hosting heavy JavaScript content unusual for business use, or domains flagged by threat intelligence feeds.
Monitor for unexpected process creation events with chrome.exe or msedge.exe as parent processes, especially spawning cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or mshta.exe.

what_to_look_for.description: Any Chrome installation below the patched version is directly vulnerable to active exploitation
Microsoft Edge running on affected Chromium engine versions is equally vulnerable

what_to_look_for.indicator: Renderer process spawning cmd.exe or powershell.exe
what_to_look_for.description: Browser renderer processes should never directly spawn command interpreters — this indicates successful sandbox escape
what_to_look_for.log_field: ParentImage contains 'chrome.exe' AND TargetImage contains 'cmd.exe' OR 'powershell.exe'

what_to_look_for.indicator: Abnormal memory access exceptions in browser process
what_to_look_for.description: Application crash logs showing out-of-bounds memory access or type confusion errors in Skia or V8 components may indicate exploitation attempts
what_to_look_for.log_field: Application Event Log: Source=Application Error, Module=chrome.dll or skia.dll

immediate_actions.description: Open Chrome, go to Help > About Google Chrome. Chrome will auto-update if it has not already. Restart Chrome after update. Verify version shows 146.0.7680.75 or higher.
immediate_actions.command:
# Check Chrome version on Linux/macOS: google-chrome --version
# Windows registry check: Get-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Google\Chrome\BLBeacon' | Select-Object version

immediate_actions.action: Update Microsoft Edge and all Chromium-based browsers
immediate_actions.description: Update Edge to 126.0.2592.68 or higher via edge://settings/help. Also update Brave, Opera, Vivaldi and any other Chromium-based browsers in your environment.
immediate_actions.command: # Check Edge version: Get-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\EdgeUpdate\Clients\{56EB18F8-B008-4CBD-B6D2-8C97FE7E9062}' | Select-Object pv

immediate_actions.action: Deploy browser updates enterprise-wide via management tools
immediate_actions.description: Use Microsoft Intune, SCCM, or Google Chrome Browser Cloud Management to push browser updates to all managed endpoints. Generate a compliance report to confirm all devices are patched.

immediate_actions.action: Enable EDR monitoring for browser process anomalies
immediate_actions.description: Configure EDR rules to alert on chrome.exe or msedge.exe spawning unexpected child processes (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, mshta.exe). This is an indicator of successful exploitation.

immediate_actions.action: Until patched: restrict browsing to approved domains
immediate_actions.description: For critical systems that cannot be immediately patched, implement web proxy allowlisting to restrict browsing to approved business domains only, reducing attack surface from drive-by compromise.
